You are an adversarial production-readiness assessor for the hc-assay engine at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (a dataset-agnostic, data-sovereign empirical-ML/methodology engine; optional backends import-guarded; transcripts committed + scrubbed intentionally). It has survived EIGHT hardening passes and CI is currently green — easy findings are gone. Report ONLY genuine, concrete, FALSIFIABLE production-readiness defects with a real failure mode and a file:line/symbol location; no style nits. If a dimension is clean, return few/zero findings — do NOT invent. Read real code (Read/Grep/Bash; "python -m pytest", "source .venv/bin/activate"). The branch is harden/pass-9 (just branched from merged main @ pass 8).

YOUR DIMENSION: pass-8 fix-regression audit.
Audit pass-8's OWN changes for follow-on defects (git log main; the K-* fixes). Scrutinize: (1) tracing.py SIGTERM re-delivery — the handler now does signal.signal(SIGTERM, SIG_DFL) + os.kill(getpid, signum); is there a re-entrancy/double-flush risk, a window where a second SIGTERM does nothing, or an issue when prior is SIG_IGN? (2) _local.py is_local_socket_path — can a crafted value like "/" alone, "/\t..", or "host=/ " bypass intent or wrongly accept a remote? does the leading-"/" check interact badly with the authority branch? (3) pipeline.py _log_trail default=str — does it now MASK a real serialization bug, or stringify something that should error? does the warning leak secrets into logs? (4) the .uv-exclude-newer scheme — is the date parsing robust (CRLF, missing file)? could a stale date silently pin known-vulnerable deps that pip-audit then flags, or hide a security update? (5) the comment-stripping diff — could it now MISS a real lock divergence (e.g. a hash change on a commented line)? (6) coverage fail_under in pyproject — does it break any `pytest` invocation that lacks --cov (fail_under with no coverage data errors)?

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

commit d08f03147df786fce18af280d398d9b51ff71feb
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 11:22:01 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
    
    Main CI had been RED (bypassed by --admin merges) on THREE counts, plus assessment
    found more. All 2-agent verified (or dispositively confirmed by the red CI logs):
    
    CI-RED (critical path to green):
    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
      collection (`No module named 'tests'`); the suite NEVER ran in CI. Fix: pythonpath
      += "." so `tests` imports under any invocation. Now collects 555 tests.
    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no
      --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was
      failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.
      Chose a cutoff (2026-06-14) clear of a release straddling the boundary (arize-phoenix
      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.
    
    CODE (assessment survivors, 2-agent verified true-positive):
    - K-REL-1 (high): SIGTERM trace-flush handler swallowed termination when prior was
      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).
      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).
    
    Regression tests added for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean;
    555 passed, 5 skipped; coverage 96.58% (floor 90). Lockfiles reproducible (script == CI regen).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	
31	
32	def _int_env(name: str, default: str) -> int:
33	    """Parse an int env var with a clear error naming the var (#CV-S-1, same class as #H-022).
34	
35	    A bare ``int(os.environ[...])`` at import time raises an opaque "invalid literal for int()"
36	    that names neither the var nor fires inside any handler.
37	    """
38	    raw = os.environ.get(name, default)
39	    try:
40	        return int(raw)
41	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
42	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be an integer; got {raw!r}") from exc
43	
44	
45	TRACING_PORT = _int_env("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006")
46	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
47	
48	_provider: Any = None
49	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
50	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
51	
52	
53	def _disabled() -> bool:
54	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
55	
56	
57	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
58	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
59	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
60	
61	
62	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
63	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
64	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
65	
66	
67	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
68	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
69	
70	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
71	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
72	    """
73	    global _provider
74	    if _disabled():
75	        return None
76	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
77	    with _provider_lock:
78	        if _provider is not None:
79	            return _provider
80	        try:
81	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
82	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
83	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
84	            from phoenix.otel import register
85	
86	            provider = register(
87	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
88	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
89	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
90	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
91	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
92	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
93	            )
94	            _provider = provider
95	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
96	            _instrument_langchain()
97	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
98	            return _provider
99	        except Exception as exc:
100	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced. Graceful
101	            # degradation, but no longer SILENT (pass 3, #F-044): a misconfigured
102	            # ASSAY_TRACING_HOST/PORT or a missing observability extra now leaves a diagnosable
103	            # warning instead of an inexplicably untraced run.
104	            _log.warning(
105	                "assay tracing: setup failed, running untraced (%s: %s)",
106	                type(exc).__name__,
107	                exc,
108	            )
109	            return None
110	
111	
112	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
113	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
114	    try:
115	        from opentelemetry import trace
116	
117	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
118	            _log.warning(
119	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
120	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
121	            )
122	    except Exception:
123	        pass
124	
125	
126	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
127	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
128	    try:
129	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
130	
131	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
132	    except Exception:
133	        pass
134	
135	
136	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
137	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
138	
139	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
140	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
141	    """
142	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
143	    if not callable(flush):
144	        return
145	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
146	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
147	        try:
148	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
149	
150	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
151	                _safe(flush)
152	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
153	                # Preserve the ORIGINAL disposition (#K-REL-1). If a Python handler pre-existed,
154	                # chain to it. Otherwise prior is SIG_DFL/SIG_IGN — both NON-callable — and simply
155	                # returning here would SWALLOW the signal: we replaced SIGTERM's default
156	                # terminate-the-process action with a flush-and-return, so `docker stop` / `kill` /
157	                # k8s would hang until SIGKILL escalation. Re-establish and re-deliver the default
158	                # action so the process still dies on SIGTERM (SIG_IGN stays ignored).
159	                if callable(prior):
160	                    prior(signum, frame)
161	                elif prior == signal.SIG_DFL:
162	                    signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, signal.SIG_DFL)
163	                    os.kill(os.getpid(), signum)  # re-deliver → default terminate action runs
164	
165	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
166	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
167	            pass
168	
169	
170	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
171	    try:
172	        fn()
173	    except Exception:
174	        pass
175	
176	
177	@contextmanager
178	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
179	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
180	
181	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
182	    """
183	    if run_id is None:
184	        yield
185	        return
186	    try:
187	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
188	    except ImportError:
189	        yield
190	        return
191	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
192	    try:
193	        yield
194	    finally:
195	        context.detach(token)
196	
197	
198	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
199	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
200	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
201	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
202	
203	
204	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
205	class Tracer(Protocol):
206	    @contextmanager
207	    def span(
208	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
209	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
210	
211	
212	class OtelTracer:
213	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
214	
215	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
216	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
217	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
218	    """
219	
220	    @contextmanager
221	    def span(
222	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
223	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
224	        try:
225	            from opentelemetry import trace
226	        except ImportError:
227	            yield
228	            return
229	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
230	        # start_as_current_span defaults record_exception=True and set_status_on_exception=True,
231	        # so an exception escaping the body is automatically recorded as a span event and the span
232	        # status is set to ERROR with the exception text (verified in test_observability) — #F-005
233	        # was a false positive; no manual error-recording is needed here.
234	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
235	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
236	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
237	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
238	            yield
239	
240	
241	class UnconfiguredTracer:
242	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
243	
244	    @contextmanager
245	    def span(
246	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
247	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
248	        raise NotImplementedError(
249	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
250	            "test double"
251	        )
252	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager

1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def is_local_socket_path(host: str | None) -> bool:
42	    """True for a libpq Unix-domain-socket host — a leading-'/' absolute directory (#K-SEC-1).
43	
44	    libpq accepts ``host=/var/run/postgresql`` (or the query form ``?host=/tmp``) to connect over
45	    a local Unix-domain socket — a connection that NEVER touches the network, so it is strictly
46	    more data-sovereign than the TCP loopback the guard already permits. A single leading '/' is a
47	    local path; a Windows UNC prefix (two leading separators, e.g. ``//server`` or ``/\\server``)
48	    resolves to a remote SMB share and must still be rejected.
49	    """
50	    if not host:
51	        return False
52	    h = host.strip()
53	    return h.startswith("/") and not h.startswith("//") and not h.startswith("/\\")
54	
55	
56	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
57	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
58	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
59	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
60	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
61	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
62	            f"from {url!r}"
63	        )
64	    return url
65	
66	
67	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
68	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
69	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
70	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
71	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
72	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
73	        )
74	    return host
75	
76	
77	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
78	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
79	
80	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority
81	    (``host1:port1,host2:port2``), so a remote second host would slip past a single-host
82	    check (audit #D3). Each host may carry its own ``:port`` and IPv6 hosts are bracketed.
83	
84	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
85	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
86	    ``[REDACTED]:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
87	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
88	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.
89	    """
90	    if "@" in netloc:
91	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]
92	    hosts: list[str] = []
93	    for part in netloc.split(","):
94	        p = part.strip()
95	        if not p:
96	            continue
97	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
98	            end = p.find("]")
99	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
100	        else:
101	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)
102	    return hosts
103	
104	
105	def require_local_uri(uri: str, *, what: str) -> str:
106	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
107	
108	    Three forms are recognized, validated against what the real client (libpq for postgres,
109	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
110	    reports, since the two can disagree:
111	
112	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
113	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host
114	      authority, #D3), AND any ``host``/``hostaddr`` query parameter that libpq would use to
115	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.
116	    - **libpq keyword/value DSN** (no authority but ``key=value`` tokens): ``host``/``hostaddr``
117	      must be loopback, tolerating libpq's whitespace/quoting around ``=`` (#P1, #D1).
118	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
119	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.
120	
121	    Inputs that cannot be parsed safely fail closed (rejected), never accepted.
122	    """
123	    try:
124	        parsed = urlparse(uri)
125	        netloc = parsed.netloc
126	        query = parsed.query
127	    except ValueError:
128	        # e.g. a malformed IPv6 authority — we cannot determine the host, so refuse.
129	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
130	            f"{what}: endpoint could not be parsed safely; rejecting (ADR-0003)"
131	        ) from None
132	
133	    if netloc:
134	        # A backslash never legitimately appears in a URI authority; a WHATWG-normalizing
135	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
136	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
137	        if "\\" in netloc:
138	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
139	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
140	            )
141	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
142	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
143	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
144	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
145	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
146	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
147	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
148	                )
149	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
150	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
151	                h = val.strip()
152	                # A Unix-domain-socket directory (leading '/') is local and accepted (#K-SEC-1).
153	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h) and not is_local_socket_path(h):
154	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
155	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
156	                        f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
157	                    )
158	        return uri
159	
160	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
161	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
162	            if match.group(1).strip().lower() in {"host", "hostaddr"}:
163	                h = match.group(2).strip().strip("'\"")
164	                # A Unix-domain-socket directory (leading '/') is local and accepted (#K-SEC-1).
165	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h) and not is_local_socket_path(h):
166	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
167	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); DSN names "
168	                        f"non-loopback host {h!r}"
169	                    )
170	        return uri
171	
172	    stripped = uri.lstrip()
173	    # Windows treats ANY two leading separators (in any mix of '\' and '/') as a UNC prefix
174	    # to a remote SMB share — \\srv, //srv, /\srv, \/srv all resolve off-box (audit #D6).
175	    # (The pure '//host' form is already caught by the authority branch above; covering it
176	    # here too keeps the branch self-contained.)
177	    if len(stripped) >= 2 and stripped[0] in "\\/" and stripped[1] in "\\/":
178	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
179	            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); UNC-style path "
180	            f"{uri!r} is a remote share"
181	        )
182	    return uri  # bare path / dir store
183

1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import datetime as _dt
32	import logging
33	import time
34	import uuid
35	import warnings
36	from contextlib import ExitStack
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from pathlib import Path
39	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping, cast
40	
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import corpus_fingerprint
43	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
44	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord, claim_set_fingerprint
45	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureMatrix
46	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
47	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode
48	from assay_engine.methodology.adjudication import (

86	class GateReview:
87	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition.
88	
89	    ``payload`` is a deep-frozen :class:`~assay_engine._frozen.FrozenDict` (immutable +
90	    hashable, #113). Because that is a ``Mapping`` and not a ``dict`` subclass, ``json.dumps``
91	    cannot serialize it directly — an operator-review handler that logs/serializes the payload
92	    must call :meth:`payload_dict` first (#141).
93	    """

95	    gate: str
96	    frm: Phase
97	    to: Phase
98	    summary: str
99	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
100	
101	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
102	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
103	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
104	
105	    def payload_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
106	        """Return the payload as a plain, deeply-thawed, JSON-serializable ``dict`` (#141)."""
107	        return cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(self.payload))

113	def _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records: list[ClaimRecord]) -> str:
114	    """Engine-computed content hash of the EXACT claim set that will be scored (#137, #F-001).
115	
116	    Delegates to the public :func:`~assay_engine.contracts.claims.claim_set_fingerprint` so the
117	    engine's authoritative fingerprint and the scheme adapters must implement in
118	    ``claim_fingerprint()`` are one and the same canonical function — the engine recomputes it
119	    over the materialized records (in scored order) and enforces agreement with the source's
120	    self-report, so an auditor re-deriving the fingerprint from the scored claims gets the value
121	    the trail records.
122	    """
123	    return claim_set_fingerprint(claim_records)

126	def _require_unique_ids(ids: list[str], what: str) -> None:
127	    """Raise FirewallViolation if ``ids`` contains a duplicate (#138).
128	
129	    Identity (claim↔hypothesis↔verdict) must be unique within a run: a repeated id would
130	    otherwise be counted once per occurrence, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing
131	    alignment_rate / verdict accounting.
132	    """
133	    seen: set[str] = set()
134	    dupes: list[str] = []
135	    for i in ids:
136	        if i in seen:
137	            dupes.append(i)
138	        seen.add(i)
139	    if dupes:
140	        raise FirewallViolation(
141	            f"duplicate {what} within one run: {sorted(set(dupes))[:3]} — identity must be "
142	            "unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator / verdict accounting)"
143	        )

146	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
147	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
148	
149	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
150	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
151	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
152	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
153	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
154	    """
155	    return GateDecision(
156	        approved=True,
157	        gate=review.gate,
158	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
159	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
160	    )

170	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the

189	                for n, v in (
190	                    ("split", self.split),
191	                    ("discover", self.discover),
192	                    ("confirm_held_out", self.confirm_held_out),

199	            missing = [
200	                n
201	                for n, v in (
202	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),

226	    def persist_trail(self, path: str | Path) -> Path:
227	        """Write the hash-chained provenance trail to ``path`` as JSON and return it (#F-045).
228	
229	        ``run_study`` keeps the trail only in memory (``self.provenance``); a caller that needs a
230	        durable, re-verifiable audit trail across process exit must persist it. The written
231	        records round-trip through :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.from_records` and re-check via
232	        :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.verify_records`. Call ``tracker.log_artifact`` on the
233	        returned path to attach it to the experiment run.
234	        """
235	        import json
236	
237	        from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
238	
239	        out = Path(path)
240	        out.write_text(
241	            json.dumps(list(entries_to_records(list(self.provenance))), indent=2),
242	            encoding="utf-8",
243	        )
244	        return out
